title: hieracium armerioides subsp. nigrellum
intro: Hieracium armerioides subsp. nigrellum, also known as False Hawk's Beard, is a lesser-known plant species in the dandelion family (Asteraceae) that is native to the Mediterranean region. Traditionally, this plant is used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Hieracium armerioides subsp. nigrellum are said to include the treatment of various ailments, such as digestive issues, respiratory problems, and skin conditions. Herbal preparations of this plant are often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
